originalBehavior: The shipped music collection spans multiple source families, but there is no single local page that turns those routes into one music-specific entry point.
localStatus: The local hub creates one curated editorial route that links only to local music replicas and local docs pages while keeping a neutral shared-footer provenance label instead of implying a single upstream original.
notes: This route is intentionally a local curation page rather than a direct one-source mirror.
The public body links only to local routes and local docs pages.
Curated multi-source provenance is documented in the local docs instead of a single external original-page link.
evidence: Smoke coverage checks the full local card count and verifies all hub hrefs stay local.
The route exposes a recommended path and cluster navigation without any external body links.

originalBehavior: The live music routes cover different goals: interval or note drills, multitrack sequencing, painted pattern writing, and genre history.
localStatus: The local hub groups those experiences into three editorial clusters and one recommended path so the music collection reads as a progression rather than a flat list.
notes: The cluster counts are 6 Teoria routes, 8 sequencing routes, and 1 genre-history route.
The recommended path now walks from ear training into beats, notes, chords, full-loop composition, and finally genre context.
evidence: The hub groups ear training and notation separately from sequencing and genre context.
Smoke coverage verifies the cluster counts and recommended-path panel remain visible on desktop and mobile widths.

originalBehavior: Custom local hub pages need their own layout and copy but still have to follow the site's shared metadata and footer contract.
localStatus: The hub reuses shared site styling, adds a bespoke warm editorial layout, and keeps the shared footer as the only provenance surface through a neutral curated-route mode.
notes: The page follows the reusable design principles without reusing the separate naming vocabulary from DESIGN.md.
No external shell, promo, or provenance links are exposed in the hub body.
evidence: The route uses shared site.css tokens plus route-local styling for the music-specific hero and clusters.
The public-surface audit keeps footer-only provenance intact for the hub route.
